Wide Range of Products and Services: HDFC Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking and financial products and services, including retail banking, corporate banking, investment banking, and wealth management. This broad range of offerings caters to the diverse needs of its customers.

Strong Market Position: HDFC Bank is one of the largest and most trusted banks in India, with a significant market share in various banking segments. Its strong market position reflects its reliability and stability, which attracts both individual and corporate clients.

Technological Innovation: HDFC Bank is known for its adoption of advanced technology and digital banking solutions. It has invested heavily in digital platforms, mobile banking apps, and online services, enhancing customer convenience and experience.

Efficient Customer Service: HDFC Bank is recognized for its efficient and customer-centric service. It has a vast network of branches, ATMs, and customer service touchpoints across the country, ensuring accessibility and responsiveness to customer needs.

Strong Financial Performance: HDFC Bank has consistently delivered strong financial performance, with robust revenue growth, profitability, and asset quality. Its sound financial health instills confidence among investors, depositors, and stakeholders.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
LiHigh Fees and Charges: HDFC Bank may have higher fees and charges compared to some other banks, especially for certain services like ATM withdrawals, non-maintenance of minimum balance, or specific transactions. This can be a concern for customers looking for more cost-effective banking options.

Overcrowded Branches and Long Wait Times: Due to its large customer base and popularity, HDFC Bank branches can sometimes be overcrowded, leading to long wait times for services such as cash withdrawals, account inquiries, or document processing. This may inconvenience customers seeking quick assistance.

Customer Service Complaints: Despite efforts to maintain efficient customer service, HDFC Bank has faced criticism in the past for delays in resolving customer queries or complaints, particularly related to issues such as disputed transactions, account closures, or loan processing. Inconsistent service levels across branches or channels may frustrate some customers.

Technology Glitches: While HDFC Bank is known for its technological innovation, it has experienced occasional technical glitches or system outages, leading to disruptions in online banking services, mobile apps, or card transactions. These incidents can inconvenience customers and erode trust in the bank's digital infrastructure.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Initially, I was fascinated by the brand HDFC because of its huge market presence and being a top among the private banks. I was curious about the selection criteria and applied for the role which was appropriate for my previous experience in the bank. It was Quess Crop who has involved in the recruitment process. The selection process was lengthy and took almost three weeks to get over. And Finally, I got a call from the recruiter saying that I got selected in the Interview process and informed me that I will be receiving the offer letter shortly. Then it took almost two weeks to receive an offer letter. In the offer Letter, it was mentioned as Personal Banker Sales with a CTC of 5LPA. At the time I was ok with the offer and accepted it through the mail. On a fine day, I received a call from HDFC Hr to confirm with me the joining date. I requested one week as breathing time as I suppose to relocate to the place which is nearby the branch which was offered to me in the offer letter. In the next week, I managed to find a place to stay and relocated myself along with my spouse. Everything was fine and good till that time. Then I called HR to confirm my joining date, it was shattering to me that my calls were ignored and no response was there. I try to communicate with HR through the mail but still i didn't receive any response from them. I got panic; I contacted the recruitment team and explained the scenario. That recruiter seriously helped a lot, she spoke to the HR team and after so many reminders and follow-ups for almost two months, the HR person revert to me and asked me whether I can join immediately or not. I confirmed in the call itself that I was immediately available to join, but it was another heart attack for me. The HR said there is only one vacancy as of now that too in the some other Branch which was almost one hour 15 mins far from the branch which was allocated previously. I was completely broken, because everything was planned and shifted by keeping the allocated branch in mind but got collapsed. I agreed to join because I had almost waited 3 months and exhausted other opportunities as well. With no other go option I was accepted to join the given branch by the next day. The job role which was offered in the Offer letter also got differs and is completely irrelevant to what they said in the Interview process. I felt like got into a trap. I joined the HDFC Santhome Branch on Oct31th. There is no proper on boarding training has been given. No one cares; I was simply sitting in the chair for the whole day. no one has taken a chance to ask you, are you? What you're doing here. Everyone was busy with their kind of stuff and I was simply staring at my mobile. One week went as it is. No training and no instructions have been given. It almost took 17 days to get the Laptop and employee Id to access the system; this shows the lethargy in the HDFC on boarding process. But the Manager expects me to do business from the day when I'm getting my employee ID itself. I could not able to cope with their process and other routines. It took almost two months for me to adjust to their work culture and start acquiring new businesses. But as soon as I entered into the third month, they started putting pressure on me. The target is unachievable and unrealistic as well. so as the work pressure is non-sense. The work culture in HDFC will be like this, every day morning there will be a Hurdle meeting to discuss yesterday's business performance and to get Today's target from everyone. The manager will blast everyone in the meeting and put one in a situation where he/she wants to end her life instead of getting humility and extreme scolding from the Manager. Every day starts with such a horrific meeting only. The whole day one has to run behind their given targets and it will not happen anyway because all the targets are unrealistic only, for example, 2 savings accounts opening, 1 Unique FD above 1 lakh and anyone loan product disbursement. at the end of the day, Manager will conduct a meeting and will ask about the achievements vs. targets given. No one will have a good day in HDFC, especially when targets are not met. Managers will ruin your whole life and make you feel that I’m fit for nothing. There is no space for mental health. Every day will be a hectic day without any good things. No one will help you because everyone will have their burden to attain the targets assigned to them and it will be always fighting between the peer mates to map the business in their names which could count in the targets achieved. If a customer enters the branch, all the staff will jump into them to catch the business. It will look like a crowd fighting to get the free food offered outside the temple. One who gets the customer will get business. That too, the staff will be in a situation like he deliberately wants to push any banking products to the attended customer to make sure that he is converting into today's business. There is no space for customer service. no one encourages complaints or service requests from the customer. They will always try to avoid those customers by saying something and even some staff will ask the customers to visit somewhere branch to get it sorted which was not the case. staff will run behind the data available and starts making calls to the customer to ask for any service requirements but it looks like asking customers whether they need any loan and wants any insurance plan literally begging the customers to cross-sell any products which will end in pushing the unnecessary products to the customers. They will always look at any calls from banks as Spam even though some customers thought the call was fraudulent. This act will ruin the relationship between the customers with the banker as well. But the staff will have no choice. Either they have to make cold calls to cross-sell or to go to the open market to distribute pamphlets or notice desperately to attain their assigned targets. People from the outside start asking why you're becoming like this, are you competing with local brokers and start humiliating the bankers actually. The bankers' situation is pathetic. Long working hours, stressful work nature, toxic work environment, non-sense targets, zero work-life balance. Even though I managed to survive in HDFC for 6 months, then I decided to quit from HDFC. I have given my resignation letter to my manager, all of sudden she started to talk very nicely and shows over care. it was like" Everything is for your good only, I’m caring for you like my child, I don't want to spoil your career. I will get a Confirmation within this week, you will get other perks and Increment and all." I come to realize that she's doing some drama to postpone my resignation as she's getting a promotion come transfer by the very next month, so the attrition rate will have an impact on her promotion it seems. She tried all possible ways to hold my resignation and finally, she did it. After so much struggle and issues I managed to leave HDFC Bank. All these things made a very bad impression on HDFC Bank on the whole. I still regret my decision on Joining HDFC. I will not recommend anyone to HDFC unless he/she is fond of making money without considering their personal life and health.
